title: Final External Synthesis
claim:
CLAIM: The hierarchical coupling law transfers to an independent
sample of N=59 SPARC galaxies. The dominant transferable channel
is VfResid (velocity residual from structural expectation), which
maintains dominance even after improving the environmental proxy.

EVIDENCE STRUCTURE:

1. HIERARCHY REPLICATES EXTERNALLY (Phase 202)
   - Core alone fails in every regime (gap < 5%)
   - VfResid dominates in every regime
   - Bootstrap P(VfResid > Core) = 100% (full & high-V)
   - 8/8 hierarchy checks passed

2. VfResid IS THE PRIMARY TRANSFER CHANNEL (Phases 201-203)
   - VfResid-only gap: 47.5% (full), 75.4% (very-high-V)
   - Partial r(VfResid, a0 | Core) = 0.844 (high-V), 0.869 (Q1+HV)
   - Channel dominance margin: +35pp to +71pp over best alternative

3. CORE IS REAL BUT SECONDARY (Phase 203)
   - Improving logMhost (r=0.232 -> 0.542) raises Core gap from
     -53% to -8.1% (full) and -6.7% to +16.7% (high-V)
   - This proves the environmental axis carries real signal
   - But VfResid still dominates by 30-76pp in every regime
   - Bootstrap P(VfResid > improved Core) = 100% (full)

4. lhOuter (5TH AXIS) REMAINS GENUINE AFTER MHOST IMPROVEMENT
   - Full: delta=+1.2pp
   - Very-High-V: delta=+8.3pp
   - Q1+HV: delta=+4.9pp
   - lhOuter contribution is NOT an artifact of crude Mhost

5. REGIME DEPENDENCE CONFIRMED
   - Signal concentrates in Vflat >= 120 km/s
   - Strongest in very-high-Vflat (>=180) and Q=1+Vflat>=120
   - Low-Vflat regime: no meaningful transfer (as expected)

REMAINING CAVEATS:
- logMhost remains a proxy (no group catalog available for N=59)
- Best estimator achieves r=0.542 with real logMhost (room to improve)
- logMR coefficient sign inconsistency persists externally
- Small-N regimes (very-high-V: N=8, Q1+HV: N=11)
paperSummary:
We test the hierarchical coupling law on N=59 independent SPARC galaxies
using frozen coefficients from the N=45 training sample. The complete
hierarchy replicates externally: Core alone fails (gap < 0 in all regimes),
VfResid dominates (P > 99.6% in all subsamples), and lhOuter provides
genuine secondary improvement (+8.3pp in very-high-Vflat).

Crucially, we decompose the role of environmental estimates by replacing
the crude logMhost formula (r=0.232 vs. tidal values) with a trained
estimator (r=0.542). Core gap improves substantially (full: -53% -> -8.1%;
high-V: -6.7% -> +16.7%), proving the environmental axis carries real
signal. However, VfResid retains clear dominance (margin 30-76pp) even
with improved environmental representation. This establishes that external
transfer is not driven mainly by the structural core, and the dominant
transferable channel is VfResid — the velocity residual from structural
expectation — independent of environmental proxy quality.
caveats: logMhost remains proxy-based (no group catalog for N=59)
Best logMhost estimator r=0.542 (room to improve with catalog data)
logMR coefficient sign inconsistency persists externally
Small-N in best regimes (very-high-V: N=8, Q1+HV: N=11)
No refitting performed — all results are pure prediction
